revision.text: #redirect[[en:Yakutsunada]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''八綱田'''（やつなた<ref>読みは『日本古代氏族人名辞典』八綱田項による。</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。'''倭日向武日向彦八綱田'''（やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた）、'''八綱多命'''とも。
[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖で、[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]第1皇子）の子とされる。
== 事績 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]第1皇子）の子であるという。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、以下の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男・倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男・八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男・倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には、豊城入彦命が上毛野君・下毛野君の祖であり、三輪山に登って東に向かい槍や刀を振り回す夢を見たと記されている。三輪山の位置する大和国[[城上郡]]には[[式内社|式内大社]]として神坐日向神社が記載されていることから、「倭日向建日向」の名はヤマト王権の東国経営に従った上毛野氏の任務を象徴するものと解されている<ref>『日本古代氏族人名辞典』八綱田項。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（[[吉川弘文館]]、2010年普及版）八綱田項
== 関連項目 == * [[毛野氏]] ** [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やつなた}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:毛野氏]]

revision.text:
'''八綱田'''（やつなた<ref>読みは『日本古代氏族人名辞典』八綱田項による。</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。'''倭日向武日向彦八綱田'''（やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた）、'''八綱多命'''とも。
[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖で、[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]第1皇子）の子とされる。
== 事績 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]第1皇子）の子であるという。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、以下の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男・倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男・八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男・倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には、豊城入彦命が上毛野君・下毛野君の祖であり、三輪山に登って東に向かい槍や刀を振り回す夢を見たと記されている。三輪山の位置する大和国[[城上郡]]には[[式内社|式内大社]]として神坐日向神社が記載されていることから、「倭日向建日向」の名はヤマト王権の東国経営に従った上毛野氏の任務を象徴するものと解されている<ref>『日本古代氏族人名辞典』八綱田項。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（[[吉川弘文館]]、2010年普及版）八綱田項
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やつなた}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝やつなた]]

'''八綱田'''（やつなた{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|1979年}}）は、[[記紀]]に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の子で、[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では「八綱田」「'''倭日向武日向彦八綱田'''（やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた）」、他文献では「八綱多命」等とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の子であるという。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男の八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には、豊城入彦命が上毛野君・下毛野君の祖であり、三輪山に登って東に向かい槍や刀を振り回す夢を見たと記されている。三輪山の位置する大和国[[城上郡]]には[[式内社|式内大社]]として神坐日向神社が記載されていることから、「倭日向建日向」の名はヤマト王権の東国経営に従った上毛野氏の任務を象徴するものと解されている{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|1979年}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=八綱田|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|八綱田（古代氏族）|1979年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やつなた}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝やつなた]]

revision.text:
'''八綱田'''（やつなた{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の子で、[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では「八綱田」「'''倭日向武日向彦八綱田'''（やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた）」、他文献では「八綱多命」等とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の子であるという。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男の八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には、豊城入彦命が上毛野君・下毛野君の祖であり、三輪山に登って東に向かい槍や刀を振り回す夢を見たと記されている。三輪山の位置する大和国[[城上郡]]には[[式内社|式内大社]]として神坐日向神社が記載されていることから、「倭日向建日向」の名はヤマト王権の東国経営に従った上毛野氏の任務を象徴するものと解されている{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=八綱田|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やつなた}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝やつなた]]

'''八綱田'''（やつなた{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。

== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の子であるという。また[[彦狭島王]]の父とされる。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男の八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
== 考証 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には、豊城入彦命が上毛野君・下毛野君の祖であり、三輪山に登って東に向かい槍や刀を振り回す夢を見たと記されている。三輪山の位置する大和国[[城上郡]]には[[式内社|式内大社]]として神坐日向神社が記載されていることから、「倭日向建日向」の名はヤマト王権の東国経営に従った上毛野氏の任務を象徴するものと解されている{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}。

== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=八綱田|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}

== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男の八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より八綱田の想像図]] '''八綱田'''（やつなた{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の子で、[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では「八綱田」「'''倭日向武日向彦八綱田'''（やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた）」、他文献では「八綱多命」等とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の子であるという。また[[彦狭島王]]の父とされる。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。

=== 宝賀寿男による異説 == 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には、豊城入彦命が上毛野君・下毛野君の祖であり、三輪山に登って東に向かい槍や刀を振り回す夢を見たと記されている。そこで、宝賀は三輪山の位置する大和国[[城上郡]]には[[式内社|式内大社]]として神坐日向神社が記載されていることから、「倭日向建日向」の名はヤマト王権の東国経営に従った上毛野氏の任務を象徴するものであるとした{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また、[[彦狭島王]]の父とされるが、[[御諸別王]]の活同年代を考えると実際には八綱田命と彦狭島王が同一人物であったと主張した<ref>「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]」『古樹紀之房間』、2005年。</ref>。

revision.text:
[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より八綱田の想像図]] '''八綱田'''（やつなた{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の子で、[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では「八綱田」「'''倭日向武日向彦八綱田'''（やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた）」、他文献では「八綱多命」等とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の子であるという。また[[彦狭島王]]の父とされる。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男の八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
=== 宝賀寿男による異説 === 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には、豊城入彦命が上毛野君・下毛野君の祖であり、三輪山に登って東に向かい槍や刀を振り回す夢を見たと記されている。そこで、宝賀は三輪山の位置する大和国[[城上郡]]には[[式内社|式内大社]]として神坐日向神社が記載されていることから、「倭日向建日向」の名はヤマト王権の東国経営に従った上毛野氏の任務を象徴するものであるとした{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また、[[彦狭島王]]の父とされるが、[[御諸別王]]の活同年代を考えると実際には八綱田命と彦狭島王が同一人物であったと主張した<ref>「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]」『古樹紀之房間』、2005年。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=八綱田|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やつなた}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝やつなた]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

revision.text:
[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より八綱田の想像図]] '''八綱田'''（やつなた{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の子で、[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では「八綱田」「'''倭日向武日向彦八綱田'''（やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた）」、他文献では「八綱多命」等とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の子であるという。また[[彦狭島王]]の父とされる。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
=== 宝賀寿男による異説 === 『日本書紀』崇神天皇段には、豊城入彦命が上毛野君・下毛野君の祖であり、三輪山に登って東に向かい槍や刀を振り回す夢を見たと記されている。そこで、宝賀は三輪山の位置する大和国[[城上郡]]には[[式内社|式内大社]]として神坐日向神社が記載されていることから、「倭日向建日向」の名はヤマト王権の東国経営に従った上毛野氏の任務を象徴するものであるとした{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また、[[彦狭島王]]の父とされるが、[[御諸別王]]の活同年代を考えると実際には八綱田命と彦狭島王が同一人物であったと主張した<ref>「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/kenu1g.htm 毛野氏族概覧]」『古樹紀之房間』、2005年。</ref>。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男の八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=八綱田|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やつなた}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝やつなた]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=八綱田|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/おっふ|おっふ]] ([[User talk:おっふ|会話]]) による ID:89476889 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]唯の個人サイトではなく、20年以上も前から続く、宝賀寿男公認の古代氏族研究会公認ＨＰ（関連の日本家系図学会ですら、最近（2021年）やっと公式HPを持てたように、古代氏族研究会の公式HPはいまだなく、このHP代理になっている）。暇つぶしのような感覚で削除されている為、復活。)

revision.text:
[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}
[[Category:Male members of the Japanese imperial family before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Kamitsukeno clan]] [[Category:Birth and death dates unknown]]
[[ja:八綱田]]

revision.text:
[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." [[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏}}

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.

The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.

He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage * Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage * Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. * Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.

The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}}== See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.

[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|ja|歌川国芳|12=draft}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|豊城入彦命|" to "|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|"
19 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:八綱田 up to rev 90633552
revision.text:
[[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より八綱田の想像図]] '''八綱田'''（やつなた{{Sfn|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]）の子で、[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]の遠祖。『[[日本書紀]]』では「八綱田」「'''倭日向武日向彦八綱田'''（やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた）」、他文献では「八綱多命」等とも表記される。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の子であるという。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男の八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=八綱田|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やつなた}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:上毛野氏|伝やつなた]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』には「[[上毛野氏|上毛野君]]遠祖」とあるのみで系譜の記載はないが、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によると[[豊城入彦命]]（[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の子であるという。
『日本書紀』垂仁天皇5年10月条によると、八綱田は[[狭穂彦王]]の反乱の際に将軍に任じられ、狭穂彦王の築いた稲城の攻撃を命じられた。八綱田は稲城に火をかけて焼き払い、狭穂彦王を自殺に追い込んだ。この功により「倭日向武日向彦八綱田」の号が授けられたという。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 皇別 ** 和泉国 登美首 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。 ** 和泉国 軽部 - 倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。[[雄略天皇]]御世、加里の郷を献上し軽部君の姓を賜った。 * 未定雑姓 ** 摂津国 我孫 - 豊城入彦命男の八綱多命の後。 ** 和泉国 我孫公 - 豊城入彦命男の倭日向建日向八綱田命の後。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=八綱田|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|八綱田（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[上毛野氏]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] {{Infobox person|image=File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|children={{ill|Q17224229|qid=Q17224229}}|father={{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|qid=Q11633375}}|native_name=八綱田}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q15275406]] Wikidata name: Yatsunata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]]'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who was a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
In the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] {{Infobox person|image=File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229}}|father={{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375}}|native_name=八綱田}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q15275406]] Wikidata name: Yatsunata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]]'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who was a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
In the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] {{Infobox person|image=File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229}}|father={{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375}}|native_name=八綱田}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q15275406]] Wikidata name: Yatsunata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]]'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who was a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
In the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] {{Infobox person|image=File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229}}|father={{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375}}|native_name=八綱田}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q15275406]] Wikidata name: Yatsunata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from [[:en:Utagawa Kuniyoshi|Utagawa Kuniyoshi]]'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who was a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
In the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}
