The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who was a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
In the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

However, according to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who was a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
In the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. == Descendant Clans ==

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who was a prince of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
In the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] {{Infobox person|image=File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|2=ja|3=彦狭島王|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|WD=Q17224229}}|father={{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375}}|native_name=八綱田}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q15275406]] Wikidata name: Yatsunata [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}

The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] {{Infobox person|image=File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|2=ja|3=彦狭島王|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|WD=Q17224229}}|father={{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375}}|native_name=八綱田}} [[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Genealogy Dictionary (Standard Edition)|year=2010|chapter=Yatsunata|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八綱田]] {{Infobox person|image=File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|children={{ill|Q17224229|WD=Q17224229|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|2=ja|3=彦狭島王|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|WD=Q17224229}}|father={{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}|native_name=八綱田}} [[File:上毛野八綱田 Kamitsukenu no Yatsunata 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|Imaginary depiction of Yatsunata from {{ill|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|en|Utagawa Kuniyoshi|WD=Q317736|lt=Utagawa Kuniyoshi|1=Utagawa Kuniyoshi}}'s ''Nihon Hyakushoden'']] '''Yatsunata''' (八綱田{{Sfn|Yatsunata (Ancient Clan)|2010}}) was a member of the ancient Japanese imperial family (royalty) as described in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and other historical records.
He was the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} (a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}) and is considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as "Yatsunata" or "'''Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata''' (やまとひむかたけひむかひこやつなた)," while other sources record him as "Yatsumata no Mikoto" and similar variants.
== Historical Records ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} mentions Yatsunata only briefly, describing him as the "distant ancestor of the Kamitsukeno clan" without detailed genealogical information. However, according to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, he is identified as the son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, who was a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}'s reign (October entry), Yatsunata was appointed as a general during the rebellion of {{ill|Sahohiko no Ō|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}. He was ordered to lead an assault on the fortress built by Sahohiko. Yatsunata set the fortress on fire, forcing Sahohiko to commit suicide. For this accomplishment, he was granted the title "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata."
== Descendant Clans ==
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists the following clans as descendants of Yatsunata: * Imperial Lineage ** Izumi Province, Tomi no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Karibe no Omi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, they offered the village of Kari and received the surname "Karibe." * Uncertain Lineage ** Settsu Province, Abiko - Descended from "Yatsumata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}. ** Izumi Province, Abiko no Kimi - Descended from "Yamato Himuka Take Himuka Hiko Yatsunata no Mikoto," son of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}.
